brief_notes: Strong neuroscience paper with clear hypothesis testing between additive and multiplicative coding models. Tables include precise R-squared values, classification accuracies, and population vector errors. The temporal transition from multiplicative to additive coding is a compelling finding. PCA-LDA classification accuracy of 88% well above chance is convincing. The RNN spontaneously developing the same coding scheme provides computational validation. Cross-subject replication across three monkeys strengthens the conclusions.
